title: Dossier quasi_circular threshold too loose: labels |0.485|-corr feature 'orthogonal'
source: loop-a-tick #14 D1/D2 (BULL_QUIET): DA medium objection — dossier lists real_yield_5y_z252d in orthogonal_features with quasi_circular:false despite correlation_with_regime_features.roc_63 = -0.485 (roc_63 is a regime-DEFINING feature).
why: The dossier's quasi_circular boolean and orthogonal_features list are consumed downstream as a signal that a feature is a clean independent edge. But a |corr|=0.485 with a regime-defining feature is not orthogonal under any reasonable threshold. The current threshold mislabels a substantially-collinear feature as orthogonal/quasi_circular=false, which can license a quasi-circular gate (momentum-on-momentum) that the hypothesizer and any downstream automation treat as legitimate. The DA caught it by reading the correlation table directly (B0043 dossier threading working), but the label itself is misleading.
value_rationale: Medium: the mislabel doesn't break anything by itself today (the DA still reads the raw correlation), but it is a latent trap for any automation that trusts the boolean, and it muddies the orthogonality contract the whole regime-gate design rests on.
recommendation: Audit the quasi_circular / orthogonal_features computation in the dossier builder (phase5.orchestrate dossiers path). Tighten the threshold so a feature with |corr| above ~0.3-0.4 against ANY regime_defining_feature is NOT placed in orthogonal_features and has quasi_circular=true (or a graded 'collinearity' score). Re-generate XAUUSD dossiers. Confirm against the tick-14 case: real_yield_5y_z252d (roc_63 -0.485) and cot_net_noncomm_z52w (roc_63 +0.442) should both lose the clean-orthogonal label.
out_of_scope: Not removing the features from availability; just correcting the orthogonality label/threshold.
